briefdescription.para: Clock is critical and will not be disabled.
Gives the max value for unsigned type Note: Not to be used with signed types.

detaileddescription.para: Parent ID is combination of parent type and node indices. Most significant 8 bits represent type of parent and least significant 8 bits represents the node index in nodes database

memberdef.name: XCLOCK_LIMIT_VALUE
XCLOCK_VALIDATE_INDEX

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if index is valid.

detaileddescription.para:
type corresponds to node type and can have either of below:
IP
OP
PLL
MUX
GATE
DIVIDER
FIXEDFACTOR
The macro assumes the index to have unsigned data type and ignores lower bound checks.

memberdef.name: XCLOCK_VALIDATE_INDEX_WARN
memberdef.initializer: do { \ if (MAX_##type < index) { \ xil_printf("Warning: Index %d out of bound for %s\n", \ index, #type); \ return; \ } \ } while (0)

briefdescription.para: This macro checks for valid index and returns no failure status.

detaileddescription.para: The macro assumes the index to have unsigned data type and ignores lower bound checks.

memberdef.name: XCLOCK_VALIDATE_PTR

memberdef.definition: typedef XStatus(* XClock_SetRateFuncPtr)(u8 NodeIdx, XClockRate ParentRate, XClockRate Rate, XClockRate *SetRate, u8 DryRun)
memberdef.argsstring: )(u8 NodeIdx, XClockRate ParentRate, XClockRate Rate, XClockRate *SetRate, u8 DryRun)
memberdef.name: XClock_SetRateFuncPtr

memberdef.definition: typedef XStatus(* XClock_SetParentFuncPtr)(u8 NodeIdx, u8 SetParentIdx)
memberdef.name: XClock_SetParentFuncPtr

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the register to read
is the pointer to variable holding read value

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful XST_FAILURE if unsuccessful
Read from register may fail in case driver doesn't have proper access at EL1 NONSECURE

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the register to write
is the 32-bit value to write to the register

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful XST_FAILURE if unsuccessful
Write to register may fail in case driver doesn't have proper access at EL1 NONSECURE.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XClockPs_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xclockps.h for the definition of XClockPs_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.
